(currently required when IPSec is enabled). In particular, this is necessary\n67: // to not block the agent bootstrap, as that prevents the scheduling of new\n68: // workloads. This logic is implemented as a separate function to avoid\n69: // forgetting to remove it when the synchronous regeneration is removed.\n70: func (r *Regenerator) CapTimeoutForSynchronousRegeneration() {\n71: \tconst maxTimeout = 5 * time.Second\n72: \tif r.cmWaitTimeout > maxTimeout {\n73: \t\tr.cmWaitTimeout = maxTimeout\n74: \t\tr.logger.WithField(logfields.Value, maxTimeout).\n75: \t\t\tInfo(\"Capped clustermesh-sync-timeout because endpoint regeneration needs to be performed synchronously\")\n76: \t}\n77: }\n78: \n79: func (r *Regenerator) WaitForKVStoreNodesSync(ctx context.Context) error {\n80: \treturn r.nodesWaitFn(ctx)\n81: }\n82: \n83: func (r *Regenerator) WaitForClusterMeshIPIdentitiesSync(ctx context.Context) error {\n84: \twctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.cmWaitTimeout)\n85: \tdefer cancel()\n86: \terr := r.cmWaitFn(wctx)\n87: \n88: \tswitch {\n89: \tcase ctx.Err() != nil:\n90: \t\t// The context associated with the endpoint has been canceled.\n91: \t\treturn ErrNotAlive\n92: \tcase err != nil:\n93: \t\t// We don't return an error in case the wait operation timed out, as we can\n94: \t\t// continue with the endpoint regeneration, although at the cost of possible\n95: \t\t// connectivity drops for cross-cluster connections. We additionally print\n96: \t\t// the warning message only once, to avoid repeating it for every endpoint.\n97: \t\tr.cmSyncLogOnce.Do(func() {\n98: \t\t\tr.logger.Warning(\"Failed waiting for clustermesh IPs and identities synchronization before regenerating endpoints, expect possible disruption of cross-cluster connections\")\n99: \t\t})\n100: \t}\n101: \n102: \treturn nil\n103: }\n104: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/cilium--v1.16.5@14bf8b70da6d30733726dd61ffc026d1ed2b187c/-/blob/pkg/endpoint/regenerator.go"}}}

suitable in\n// case the regeneration of an endpoint needs to be performed synchronously\n// (currently required when IPSec is enabled). In particular, this is necessary\n// to not block the agent bootstrap, as that prevents the scheduling of new\n// workloads. This logic is implemented as a separate function to avoid\n// forgetting to remove it when the synchronous regeneration is removed.\nfunc (r *Regenerator) CapTimeoutForSynchronousRegeneration() {\n\tconst maxTimeout = 5 * time.Second\n\tif r.cmWaitTimeout > maxTimeout {\n\t\tr.cmWaitTimeout = maxTimeout\n\t\tr.logger.WithField(logfields.Value, maxTimeout).\n\t\t\tInfo(\"Capped clustermesh-sync-timeout because endpoint regeneration needs to be performed synchronously\")\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (r *Regenerator) WaitForKVStoreNodesSync(ctx context.Context) error {\n\treturn r.nodesWaitFn(ctx)\n}\n\nfunc (r *Regenerator) WaitForClusterMeshIPIdentitiesSync(ctx context.Context) error {\n\twctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.cmWaitTimeout)\n\tdefer cancel()\n\terr := r.cmWaitFn(wctx)\n\n\tswitch {\n\tcase ctx.Err() != nil:\n\t\t// The context associated with the endpoint has been canceled.\n\t\treturn ErrNotAlive\n\tcase err != nil:\n\t\t// We don't return an error in case the wait operation timed out, as we can\n\t\t// continue with the endpoint regeneration, although at the cost of possible\n\t\t// connectivity drops for cross-cluster connections. We additionally print\n\t\t// the warning message only once, to avoid repeating it for every endpoint.\n\t\tr.cmSyncLogOnce.Do(func() {\n\t\t\tr.logger.Warning(\"Failed waiting for clustermesh IPs and identities synchronization before regenerating endpoints, expect possible disruption of cross-cluster connections\")\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// RegenerateAllEndpoints calls a setState for each endpoint and\n// regenerates if state transaction is valid. During this process, the endpoint\n// list is locked and cannot be modified.\n// Returns a waiting group that can be used to know when all the endpoints are\n// regenerated.\nfunc (er *EndpointRegenerator) RegenerateAllEndpoints(regenMetadata *regeneration.ExternalRegenerationMetadata) *sync.WaitGroup {\n\tvar wg sync.WaitGroup\n\n\teps := er.source.GetEndpoints()\n\twg.Add(len(eps))\n\n\t// Dereference \"reason\" field outside of logging statement; see\n\t// https://github.com/sirupsen/logrus/issues/1003.\n\treason := regenMetadata.Reason\n\ter.logger.WithField(\"reason\", reason).Info(\"regenerating all endpoints\")\n\tfor _, ep := range eps {\n\t\tgo func(ep *Endpoint) {\n\t\t\t<-ep.RegenerateIfAlive(regenMetadata)\n\t\t\twg.Done()\n\t\t}(ep)\n\t}\n\n\treturn &wg\n}\n\n// WaitForEndpointsAtPolicyRev waits for all endpoints which existed at the time\n// this function is called to be at a given policy revision.\n// New endpoints appearing while waiting are ignored.\nfunc (er *EndpointRegenerator) WaitForEndpointsAtPolicyRev(ctx context.Context, rev uint64) error {\n\teps := er.source.GetEndpoints()\n\tfor i := range eps {\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n\t\t\treturn ctx.Err()\n\t\tcase <-eps[i].WaitForPolicyRevision(ctx, rev, nil):\n\t\t\tif ctx.Err() != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn ctx.Err()\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}

\\n49: \\t// mutex protects endpoints and endpointsAux\\n50: \\tmutex lock.RWMutex\\n51: \\n52: \\t// endpoints is the global list of endpoints indexed by ID. mutex must\\n53: \\t// be held to read and write.\\n54: \\tendpoints    map[uint16]*endpoint.Endpoint\\n55: \\tendpointsAux map[string]*endpoint.Endpoint\\n56: \\n57: \\t// mcastManager handles IPv6 multicast group join/leave for pods. This is required for the\\n58: \\t// node to receive ICMPv6 NDP messages, especially NS (Neighbor Solicitation) message, so\\n59: \\t// pod's IPv6 address is discoverable.\\n60: \\tmcastManager *mcastmanager.MCastManager\\n61: \\n62: \\t// EndpointSynchronizer updates external resources (e.g., Kubernetes) with\\n63: \\t// up-to-date information about endpoints managed by the endpoint manager.\\n64: \\tEndpointResourceSynchronizer\\n65: \\n66: \\t// subscribers are notified when events occur in the endpointManager.\\n67: \\tsubscribers map[Subscriber]struct{}\\n68: \\n69: \\t// checkHealth supports endpoint garbage collection by verifying the health\\n70: \\t// of an endpoint.\\n71: \\tcheckHealth EndpointCheckerFunc\\n72: \\n73: \\t// deleteEndpoint is the function used to remove the endpoint from the\\n74: \\t// endpointManager and clean it up. Always set to RemoveEndpoint.\\n75: \\tdeleteEndpoint endpointDeleteFunc\\n76: \\n77: \\t// A mark-and-sweep garbage collector may operate on the endpoint list.\\n78: \\t// This is configured via WithPeriodicEndpointGC() and will mark\\n79: \\t// endpoints for removal on one run of the controller, then in the\\n80: \\t// subsequent controller run will remove the endpoints.\\n81: {\\n633: \\t\\treturn err\\n634: \\t}\\n635: \\tmgr.mutex.RLock()\\n636: \\t// Unlock the mutex after reading the subscribers list to not block\\n637: \\t// endpoint restore operation. identifiers[endpointid.CNIAttachmentIdPrefix],\\n407: \\t\\t\\t\\tlogfields.CEPName:         identifiers[endpointid.CEPNamePrefix],\\n408: \\t\\t\\t}).Warning(\\\"Unable to release endpoint ID\\\")\\n409: \\t\\t}\\n410: \\t}\\n411: \\n412: \\tmgr.removeReferencesLocked(identifiers)\\n413: }\\n414: \\n415: // removeEndpoint stops the active handling of events by the specified endpoint,\\n416: // and prevents the endpoint from being globally acccessible via other packages.\\n417: func (mgr *endpointManager) removeEndpoint(ep *endpoint.Endpoint, conf endpoint.DeleteConfig) []error {\\n418: \\tmgr.unexpose(ep)\\n419: \\tresult := ep.Delete(conf)\\n420: \\n421: \\tmgr.mutex.RLock()\\n422: \\tfor s := range mgr.subscribers {\\n423: \\t\\ts.EndpointDeleted(ep, conf)\\n424: endpointManager struct {\n47: \thealth cell.Health\n48: \n49: \t// mutex protects endpoints and endpointsAux\n50: \tmutex lock.RWMutex\n51: \n52: \t// endpoints is the global list of endpoints indexed by ID. mutex must\n53: \t// be held to read and write.\n54: \tendpoints    map[uint16]*endpoint.Endpoint\n55: \tendpointsAux map[string]*endpoint.Endpoint\n56: \n57: \t// mcastManager handles IPv6 multicast group join/leave for pods. This is required for the\n58: \t// node to receive ICMPv6 NDP messages, especially NS (Neighbor Solicitation) message, so\n59: \t// pod's IPv6 address is discoverable.\n60: \tmcastManager *mcastmanager.MCastManager\n61: \n62: \t// EndpointSynchronizer updates external resources (e.g., Kubernetes) with\n63: \t// up-to-date information about endpoints managed by the endpoint manager.\n64: \tEndpointResourceSynchronizer\n65: \n66: \t// subscribers are notified when events occur in the endpointManager.\n67: \tsubscribers map[Subscriber]struct{}\n68: \n69: \t// checkHealth supports endpoint garbage collection by verifying the health\n70: \t// of an endpoint.\n71: \tcheckHealth EndpointCheckerFunc\n72: \n73: \t// deleteEndpoint is the function used to remove the endpoint from the\n74: \t// endpointManager and clean it up. Always set to RemoveEndpoint.\n75: \tdeleteEndpoint endpointDeleteFunc\n76: \n77: \t// A mark-and-sweep garbage collector may operate on the endpoint list.\n78: \t// This is configured via WithPeriodicEndpointGC() and will mark\n79: \t// endpoints for removal on one run of the controller, then in the\n80: \t// subsequent controller run will remove the endpoints.\n81: \tmarkedEndpoints []uint16\n82: \n83: \t// controllers associated with the endpoint manager.\n84: \tcontrollers *controller.Manager\n85: \n86: \tpolicyMapPressure *policyMapPressure\n87: \n88: \t// locaNodeStore allows *epIDAllocator\n94: }\n95: \n96: // endpointDeleteFunc is used to abstract away concrete Endpoint Delete\n97: // functionality from endpoint management for testing purposes.\n98: type wait on the 'notifyWg' parameter before updating policy maps.\n155: func (mgr *endpointManager) UpdatePolicyMaps(ctx context.Context, notifyWg *sync.WaitGroup) *sync.WaitGroup {\n156: \tvar epWG *endpointManager) RestoreEndpoint(ep *endpoint.Endpoint) error {\n630: \tep.SetDefaultConfiguration()\n631: \terr := mgr.expose(ep)\n632: \tif err != nil {\n633: \t\treturn err\n634: \t}\n635: \tmgr.mutex.RLock()\n636: \t// Unlock the mutex after reading the subscribers list to not block\n637: \t// endpoint restore operation. This could potentially mean that\n638: \t// subscribers are called even after they've unsubscribed. However,\n639: \t// consumers unsubscribe during the tear down phase so the restore\n640: \t// callbacks may likely not race with unsubscribe calls.\n641: \tsubscribers := *endpointManager) initHostEndpointLabels(ctx context.Context, ep *endpoint.Endpoint) {\n745: \t// initHostEndpointLabels is executed by the daemon start hook, and\n746: \t// at that point we are associated with the endpoint manager.\n\tcontrollers *controller.Manager\n\n\tpolicyMapPressure *policyMapPressure\n\n\t// locaNodeStore allows to retrieve information and observe changes about\n\t// logic.\n\tregenerator *endpoint.EndpointRegenerator\n}\n\n// endpointDeleteFunc is used to abstract away concrete Endpoint Delete\n// functionality from endpoint management for testing purposes.\ntype endpointDeleteFunc func(*endpoint.Endpoint, endpoint.DeleteConfig) []error\n\n// New creates a new endpointManager.\nfunc New(epSynchronizer EndpointResourceSynchronizer, lns *node.LocalNodeStore, health cell.Health) *endpointManager {\n\tmgr := endpointManager{\n\t\thealth:                       health,\n\t\tendpoints:                    make(map[uint16]*endpoint.Endpoint),\n\t\tendpointsAux:                 make(map[string]*endpoint.Endpoint),\n\t\tmcastManager:                 mcastmanager.New(option.Config.IPv6MCastDevice),\n\t\tEndpointResourceSynchronizer: EndpointCheckerFunc.\nfunc (mgr *endpointManager) WithPeriodicEndpointGC(ctx context.Context, checkHealth EndpointCheckerFunc, interval time.Duration) *endpointManager {\n\tmgr.checkHealth = checkHealth\n\tmgr.controllers.UpdateController(\"endpoint-gc\",\n\t\tcontroller.ControllerParams{\n\t\t\tGroup:       endpointGCControllerGroup,\n\t\t\tDoFunc: all proxy changes have been completed.\nfunc waitForProxyCompletions(proxyWaitGroup *completion.WaitGroup) error {\n\terr := proxyWaitGroup.Context().Err()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"context cancelled before waiting for proxy updates: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tstart := time.Now()\n\tlog.Debug(\"Waiting for proxy updates to complete...\")\n\terr = proxyWaitGroup.Wait()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"proxy updates failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tlog.Debug(\"Wait time for proxy updates: \", time.Since(start))\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// UpdatePolicyMaps returns a WaitGroup which is signaled upon once all endpoints\n// have had their PolicyMaps updated against the Endpoint's desired policy state.\n//\n// Endpoints will wait on the 'notifyWg' parameter before updating policy maps.\nfunc (mgr *endpointManager) UpdatePolicyMaps(ctx context.Context, notifyWg *sync.WaitGroup) *sync.WaitGroup {\n\tvar epWG sync.WaitGroup\n\tvar wg sync.WaitGroup\n\n\tproxyWaitGroup := completion.NewWaitGroup(ctx)\n\n\teps := mgr.GetEndpoints()\n\tepWG.Add(len(eps))\n\twg.Add(1)\n\n\t// This is in a goroutine to allow the caller to proceed with other tasks before waiting for the ACKs to complete\n\tgo func() {\n\t\t// Wait for all the eps to have applied policy map\n\t\t// changes before waiting for the changes to be ACKed\n\t\tepWG.Wait()\n\t\tif err := waitForProxyCompletions(proxyWaitGroup); err != nil {\n\t\t\tlog.WithError(err).Warning(\"Failed to apply L7 proxy policy changes. These will be re-applied in future updates.\")\n\t\t}\n\t\twg.Done()\n\t}()\n\n\t// TODO: bound by number of CPUs?\n\tfor _, ep := range eps {\n\t\tgo func(ep *endpoint.Endpoint) {\n\t\t\t// Proceed only after all notifications have been delivered to endpoints\n\t\t\tnotifyWg.Wait()\n\t\t\tif err := ep.ApplyPolicyMapChanges(proxyWaitGroup); err != nil && !errors.Is(err, endpoint.ErrNotAlive) {\n\t\t\t\tep.Logger(\"endpointmanager\").WithError(err).Warning(\"Failed to apply policy map changes. These will be re-applied in future updates.\")\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tepWG.Done()\n\t\t}(ep)\n\t}\n\n\treturn &wg\n}\n\n// InitMetrics hooks the endpointManager into the metrics subsystem. This can\n// only be done once, globally, otherwise the metrics library will panic.\nfunc (mgr *endpointManager) InitMetrics(registry *metrics.Registry) {\n\tif option.Config.DryMode {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tmetricsOnce.Do(func() { // Endpoint is a function used to collect this metric. We cannot\n\t\t// increment/decrement a gauge since we invoke Remove gratuitously and that\n\t\t// would result in negative counts.\n\t\t// It must be thread-safe.\n\n\t\tmetrics.Endpoint = metric.NewGaugeFunc(metric.GaugeOpts{\n\t\t\tNamespace: metrics.Namespace,\n\t\t\tName:      \"endpoint\",\n\t\t\tHelp:      \"Number of endpoints managed by this agent\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t\tfunc() float64 { return float64(len(mgr.GetEndpoints())) },\n\t\t)\n\t\tregistry.MustRegister(metrics.Endpoint)\n\t})\n}\n\n// allocateID checks if the ID can be reused. If it cannot, returns an error.\n// If an ID of 0 is provided, a new ID is allocated. If a new ID cannot be\n// allocated, returns an error.\nfunc (mgr *endpointManager) allocateID(currID uint16) (uint16, error) {\n\tvar newID uint16\n\tif currID != 0 {\n\t\tif err := mgr.epIDAllocator.reuse(currID); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn 0, fmt.Errorf(\"unable to reuse endpoint ID: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t\tnewID = currID\n\t} else {\n\t\tid := mgr.epIDAllocator.allocate()\n\t\tif id == uint16(0) {\n\t\t\treturn 0, fmt.Errorf(\"no more endpoint IDs available\")\n\t\t}\n\t\tnewID = id\n\t}\n\n\treturn newID, nil\n}\n\nfunc (mgr *endpointManager) removeIDLocked(currID uint16) {\n\tdelete(mgr.endpoints, currID)\n}\n\n// RemoveID removes the id from the endpoints map in the endpointManager.\nfunc (mgr *endpointManager) RemoveID(currID uint16) {\n\tmgr.mutex.Lock()\n\tdefer mgr.mutex.Unlock()\n\tmgr.removeIDLocked(currID)\n}\n\n// Lookup looks up the endpoint by prefix id\nfunc (mgr *endpointManager) Lookup(id string) (*endpoint.Endpoint, error) {\n\tmgr.mutex.RLock()\n\tdefer mgr.mutex.RUnlock()\n\n\tprefix, eid, err := endpointid.Parse(id)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\tswitch prefix {\n\tcase endpointid.CiliumLocalIdPrefix:\n\t\tn, err := endpointid.ParseCiliumID(id)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\t\tif n > endpointid.MaxEndpointID {\n\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"%d: endpoint ID too large\", n)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn mgr.lookupCiliumID(uint16(n)), nil\n\n\tcase endpointid.CiliumGlobalIdPrefix:\n\t\treturn nil, ErrUnsupportedID\n\n\tcase endpointid.CNIAttachmentIdPrefix:\n\t\treturn mgr.lookupCNIAttachmentID(eid), nil\n\n\tcase endpointid.ContainerIdPrefix:\n\t\treturn mgr.lookupContainerID(eid), nil\n\n\tcase endpointid.DockerEndpointPrefix:\n\t\treturn mgr.lookupDockerEndpoint(eid), nil\n\n\tcase endpointid.ContainerNamePrefix:\n\t\treturn mgr.lookupDockerContainerName(eid), nil\n\n\tcase endpointid.PodNamePrefix:\n\t\treturn mgr.lookupPodNameLocked(eid), nil\n\n\tcase endpointid.CEPNamePrefix:\n\t\treturn mgr.lookupCEPNameLocked(eid), nil\n\n\tcase endpointid.IPv4Prefix:\n\t\treturn mgr.lookupIPv4(eid), nil\n\n\tcase endpointid.IPv6Prefix:\n\t\treturn mgr.lookupIPv6(eid), nil\n\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn nil, ErrInvalidPrefix{InvalidPrefix: prefix.String()}\n\t}\n}\n\n// LookupCiliumID looks up endpoint by endpoint ID\nfunc (mgr *endpointManager) LookupCiliumID(id uint16) *endpoint.Endpoint {\n\tmgr.mutex.RLock()\n\tep := mgr.lookupCiliumID(id)\n\tmgr.mutex.RUnlock()\n\treturn ep\n}\n\n// LookupCNIAttachmentID looks up endpoint by CNI attachment ID\nfunc (mgr *endpointManager) LookupCNIAttachmentID(id string) *endpoint.Endpoint {\n\tmgr.mutex.RLock()\n\tep := mgr.lookupCNIAttachmentID(id)\n\tmgr.mutex.RUnlock()\n\treturn ep\n}\n\n// LookupIPv4 looks up endpoint by IPv4 address\nfunc (mgr *endpointManager) LookupIPv4(ipv4 string) *endpoint.Endpoint {\n\tmgr.mutex.RLock()\n\tep := mgr.lookupIPv4(ipv4)\n\tmgr.mutex.RUnlock()\n\treturn ep\n}\n\n// LookupIPv6 looks up endpoint by IPv6 address\nfunc (mgr *endpointManager) LookupIPv6(ipv6 string) *endpoint.Endpoint {\n\tmgr.mutex.RLock()\n\tep := mgr.lookupIPv6(ipv6)\n\tmgr.mutex.RUnlock()\n\treturn ep\n}\n\n// LookupIP looks up endpoint by IP address\nfunc (mgr *endpointManager) LookupIP(ip netip.Addr) (ep *endpoint.Endpoint) {\n\tipStr := ip.Unmap().String()\n\tmgr.mutex.RLock()\n\tif ip.Is4() {\n\t\tep = mgr.lookupIPv4(ipStr)\n\t} else {\n\t\tep = mgr.lookupIPv6(ipStr)\n\t}\n\tmgr.mutex.RUnlock()\n\treturn ep\n}\n\n// LookupCEPName looks up an endpoint by its K8s namespace + cep name\nfunc (mgr *endpointManager) LookupCEPName(namespacedName string) *endpoint.Endpoint {\n\tmgr.mutex.RLock()\n\tep := mgr.lookupCEPNameLocked(namespacedName)\n\tmgr.mutex.RUnlock()\n\treturn ep\n}\n\n// GetEndpointsByPodName looks up endpoints by namespace + pod name\nfunc (mgr *endpointManager) GetEndpointsByPodName(namespacedName string) []*endpoint.Endpoint {\n\tmgr.mutex.RLock()\n\tdefer mgr.mutex.RUnlock()\n\teps := make([]*endpoint.Endpoint, 0, 1)\n\tfor _, ep := range mgr.endpoints {\n\t\tif ep.GetK8sNamespaceAndPodName() == namespacedName {\n\t\t\teps = append(eps, ep)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn eps\n}\n\n// mgr.mutex.RUnlock()\n\n\teps := make([]*endpoint.Endpoint, 0, 1)\n\tfor _, ep := range mgr.endpoints {\n\t\tif ep.GetContainerID() == containerID {\n\t\t\teps = append(eps, ep)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn acccessible via other packages.\nfunc (mgr *endpointManager) removeEndpoint(ep *endpoint.Endpoint, conf endpoint.DeleteConfig) []error {\n\tmgr.unexpose(ep)\n\tresult := {\n\thealth cell.Health\n\n\t// mutex protects endpoints and endpointsAux\n\tmutex lock.RWMutex\n\n\t// endpoints is the global list of endpoints indexed by ID. mutex must\n\t// be held to read and write.\n\tendpoints    map[uint16]*endpoint.Endpoint\n\tendpointsAux map[string]*endpoint.Endpoint\n\n\t// mcastManager handles IPv6 multicast group join/leave for pods. This is required for the\n\t// node to receive ICMPv6 NDP messages, especially NS (Neighbor Solicitation) message, so\n\t// pod's IPv6 address is discoverable.\n\tmcastManager *mcastmanager.MCastManager\n\n\t// EndpointSynchronizer updates external resources (e.g., Kubernetes) with\n\t// up-to-date information about endpoints managed by the endpoint manager.\n\tEndpointResourceSynchronizer\n\n\t// subscribers are notified when events occur in the endpointManager.\n\tsubscribers map[Subscriber]struct{}\n\n\t// checkHealth supports endpoint garbage collection by verifying the health\n\t// of an endpoint.\n\tcheckHealth EndpointCheckerFunc\n\n\t// deleteEndpoint is the function used to remove the endpoint from the\n\t// endpointManager and clean it up. Always set to RemoveEndpoint.\n\tdeleteEndpoint endpointDeleteFunc\n\n\t// A mark-and-sweep garbage collector may operate on the endpoint list.\n\t// This is configured via WithPeriodicEndpointGC() and will mark\n\t// endpoints for removal on one run of the controller, then in the\n\t// subsequent controller run will remove the endpoints.\n\tmarkedEndpoints []uint16\n\n\t// controllers associated with the endpoint manager.\n\tcontrollers *controller.Manager\n\n\tpolicyMapPressure *policyMapPressure\n\n\t// locaNodeStore allows to retrieve information and observe changes about\n\t// logic.\n\tregenerator *endpoint.EndpointRegenerator\n}\n\n// endpointDeleteFunc is used to abstract away concrete Endpoint Delete\n// functionality from endpoint management for testing purposes.\ntype endpointDeleteFunc func(*endpoint.Endpoint, endpoint.DeleteConfig) []error\n\n// New creates a new endpointManager.\nfunc New(epSynchronizer EndpointResourceSynchronizer, lns *node.LocalNodeStore, health cell.Health) *endpointManager {\n\tmgr := endpointManager{\n\t\thealth:                       health,\n\t\tendpoints:                    make(map[uint16]*endpoint.Endpoint),\n\t\tendpointsAux:                 make(map[string]*endpoint.Endpoint),\n\t\tmcastManager:                 mcastmanager.New(option.Config.IPv6MCastDevice),\n\t\tEndpointResourceSynchronizer: all proxy changes have been completed.\nfunc waitForProxyCompletions(proxyWaitGroup *completion.WaitGroup) error {\n\terr := proxyWaitGroup.Context().Err()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"context cancelled before waiting for proxy updates: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tstart := time.Now()\n\tlog.Debug(\"Waiting for proxy updates to complete...\")\n\terr = proxyWaitGroup.Wait()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"proxy updates failed: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tlog.Debug(\"Wait time for proxy updates: \", time.Since(start))\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// 'notifyWg' parameter before updating policy maps.\nfunc (mgr *endpointManager) UpdatePolicyMaps(ctx context.Context, notifyWg *sync.WaitGroup) *sync.WaitGroup {\n\tvar epWG sync.WaitGroup\n\tvar wg sync.WaitGroup\n\n\tproxyWaitGroup := completion.NewWaitGroup(ctx)\n\n\teps := mgr.GetEndpoints()\n\tepWG.Add(len(eps))\n\twg.Add(1)\n\n\t// This is in a goroutine to allow the caller to proceed with other tasks before waiting for the ACKs to complete\n\tgo func() {\n\t\t// Wait for all the eps to have applied policy map\n\t\t// changes before waiting for the changes to be ACKed\n\t\tepWG.Wait()\n\t\tif err := waitForProxyCompletions(proxyWaitGroup); err != nil {\n\t\t\tlog.WithError(err).Warning(\"Failed to apply L7 proxy policy changes. These will be re-applied in future updates.\")\n\t\t}\n\t\twg.Done()\n\t}()\n\n\t// TODO: bound by number of CPUs?\n\tfor _, ep := range eps {\n\t\tgo func(ep *endpoint.Endpoint) {\n\t\t\t// Proceed only after all notifications have been delivered to endpoints\n\t\t\tnotifyWg.Wait()\n\t\t\tif err := ep.ApplyPolicyMapChanges(proxyWaitGroup); err != nil && !errors.Is(err, endpoint.ErrNotAlive) {\n\t\t\t\tep.Logger(\"endpointmanager\").WithError(err).Warning(\"Failed to apply policy map changes. These will be re-applied in future updates.\")\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tepWG.Done()\n\t\t}(ep)\n\t}\n\n\treturn &wg\n}\n\n// InitMetrics hooks the endpointManager into the metrics subsystem. This can\n// only be done once, globally, otherwise the metrics library will panic.\nfunc (mgr *endpointManager) InitMetrics(registry *metrics.Registry) {\n\tif option.Config.DryMode {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tmetricsOnce.Do(func() { // Endpoint is a function used to collect this metric. We cannot\n\t\t// increment/decrement a gauge since we invoke Remove gratuitously and that\n\t\t// would result in negative counts.\n\t\t// It must be thread-safe.\n\n\t\tmetrics.Endpoint = metric.NewGaugeFunc(metric.GaugeOpts{\n\t\t\tNamespace: metrics.Namespace,\n\t\t\tName:      \"endpoint\",\n\t\t\tHelp:      \"Number of endpoints managed by this agent\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t\tfunc() float64 { return float64(len(mgr.GetEndpoints())) },\n\t\t)\n\t\tregistry.MustRegister(metrics.Endpoint)\n\t})\n}\n\n// allocateID checks if the ID can be reused. If it cannot, returns an error.\n// If an ID of 0 is provided, a new ID is allocated. If a new ID cannot be\n// allocated, returns an error.\nfunc (mgr *endpointManager) allocateID(currID uint16) (uint16, error) {\n\tvar newID uint16\n\tif currID != 0 {\n\t\tif err := mgr.epIDAllocator.reuse(currID); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn 0, fmt.Errorf(\"unable to reuse endpoint ID: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t\tnewID = currID\n\t} else {\n\t\tid := mgr.epIDAllocator.allocate()\n\t\tif id == uint16(0) {\n\t\t\treturn 0, fmt.Errorf(\"no more endpoint IDs available\")\n\t\t}\n\t\tnewID = id\n\t}\n\n\treturn newID, nil\n}\n\nfunc (mgr *endpointManager) removeIDLocked(currID uint16) {\n\tdelete(mgr.endpoints, currID)\n}\n\n// RemoveID removes the id from the endpoints map in the endpointManager.\nfunc (mgr *endpointManager) RemoveID(currID uint16) {\n\tmgr.mutex.Lock()\n\tdefer mgr.mutex.Unlock()\n\tmgr.removeIDLocked(currID)\n}\n\n// Lookup looks up the endpoint by prefix id\nfunc (mgr *endpointManager) Lookup(id {\n\tcase endpointid.CiliumLocalIdPrefix:\n\t\tn, err := endpointid.ParseCiliumID(id)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\t\tif n > endpointid.MaxEndpointID {\n\t\t\treturn nil, mgr.lookupIPv4(eid), nil\n\n\tcase endpointid.IPv6Prefix:\n\t\treturn mgr.lookupIPv6(eid), nil\n\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn nil, ErrInvalidPrefix{InvalidPrefix: prefix.String()}\n\t}\n}\n\n// *endpoint.Endpoint {\n\tmgr.mutex.RLock()\n\tep := mgr.lookupCNIAttachmentID(id)\n\tmgr.mutex.RUnlock()\n\treturn ep\n}\n\n// LookupIPv4 looks up endpoint by IPv4 address\nfunc (mgr *endpointManager) endpoint.\nfunc (mgr *endpointManager) unexpose(ep *endpoint.Endpoint) {\n\tdefer ep.Close()\n\tidentifiers := ep.Identifiers()\n\n\tpreviousState := ep.GetState()\n\n\tmgr.mutex.Lock()\n\tdefer Do not GC these endpoints this round.\\n22: // 2) Sweep all endpoints marked as requiring GC during the previous iteration.\\n23: //\\n24: // This way, if there is a temporary condition that will be resolved by other\\n25: // components in the system, then we will not flag warnings about the system\\n26: // getting out-of-sync.\\n27: func (mgr *endpointManager) markAndSweep(ctx context.Context) error {\\n28: \\tmarked := mgr.markEndpoints()\\n29: \\n30: \\tmgr.mutex.Lock()\\n31: \\ttoSweep := mgr.markedEndpoints\\n32: \\tmgr.markedEndpoints = marked\\n33: \\tmgr.mutex.Unlock()\\n34: \\n35: \\t// Avoid returning an error which would cause the calling controller to\\n36: \\t// re-run the garbage collection more frequently than the RunInterval.\\n37: \\tmgr.sweepEndpoints(toSweep)\\n38: \\treturn nil\\n39: }\\n40: \\n41: // markEndpoints runs all endpoints in the manager against the configured\\n42: // EndpointChecker and returns a slice of endpoint ids that require garbage\\n43: // collection.\\n44: func (mgr *endpointManager) markEndpoints() []uint16 {\\n45: \\tmgr.mutex.RLock()\\n46: \\tdefer mgr.mutex.RUnlock()\\n47: \\n48: \\tneedsGC := make([]uint16, 0, len(mgr.endpoints))\\n49: \\tfor eid, ep := range mgr.endpoints {\\n50: \\t\\tif err := mgr.checkHealth(ep); err != nil {\\n51: \\t\\t\\tneedsGC = append(needsGC, eid)\\n52: \\t\\t}\\n53: \\t}\\n54: \\treturn needsGC\\n55: }\\n56: \\n57: // sweepEndpoints iterates through the specified list of endpoints marked for\\n58: // deletion and attempts to garbage-collect them if they still exist.\\n59: func (mgr *endpointManager) sweepEndpoints(markedEndpoints []uint16) {\\n60: \\ttoSweep := make([]*endpoint.Endpoint, 0, len(markedEndpoints))\\n61: \\n62: \\t// 'markedEndpoints' were marked during the previous mark round, so\\n63: \\t// they may no longer be valid endpoints. Narrow the list to only the\\n64: \\t// endpoints that remain. Do not GC these endpoints this round.\\n22: // 2) Sweep all endpoints marked as requiring GC during the previous iteration.\\n23: //\\n24: // This way, if there is a temporary condition that will be resolved by other\\n25: // components in the system, then we will not flag warnings about the system\\n26: // getting out-of-sync.\\n27: func (mgr *endpointManager) markAndSweep(ctx context.Context) error {\\n28: \\tmarked := mgr.markEndpoints()\\n29: \\n30: \\tmgr.mutex.Lock()\\n31: \\ttoSweep := mgr.markedEndpoints\\n32: \\tmgr.markedEndpoints = marked\\n33: \\tmgr.mutex.Unlock()\\n34: \\n35: \\t// Avoid returning an error which would cause the calling controller to\\n36: \\t// re-run the garbage collection more frequently than the RunInterval.\\n37: \\tmgr.sweepEndpoints(toSweep)\\n38: \\treturn nil\\n39: }\\n40: \\n41: // markEndpoints runs all endpoints in the manager against the configured\\n42: // EndpointChecker and returns a slice of endpoint ids that require garbage\\n43: // collection.\\n44: func (mgr *endpointManager) markEndpoints() []uint16 {\\n45: \\tmgr.mutex.RLock()\\n46: \\tdefer mgr.mutex.RUnlock()\\n47: \\n48: \\tneedsGC := make([]uint16, 0, len(mgr.endpoints))\\n49: \\tfor eid, ep := range mgr.endpoints {\\n50: \\t\\tif err := mgr.checkHealth(ep); err != nil {\\n51: \\t\\t\\tneedsGC = append(needsGC, eid)\\n52: \\t\\t}\\n53: \\t}\\n54: \\treturn needsGC\\n55: }\\n56: \\n57: // sweepEndpoints iterates through the specified list of endpoints marked for\\n58: // deletion and attempts to garbage-collect them if they still exist.\\n59: func (mgr *endpointManager) sweepEndpoints(markedEndpoints []uint16) {\\n60: \\ttoSweep := make([]*endpoint.Endpoint, 0, len(markedEndpoints))\\n61: \\n62: \\t// 'markedEndpoints' were marked during the previous mark round, so\\n63: \\t// they may no longer be valid endpoints. Narrow the list to only the\\n64: \\t// endpoints that remain. Then, release the lock so RemoveEndpoint()\\n65: \\t// below can independently grab it.\\n66: \\tmgr.mutex.RLock()\\n67: \\tfor _, id := range markedEndpoints {\\n68: \\t\\tif ep, ok := mgr.endpoints[id]; ok {\\n69: \\t\\t\\ttoSweep = append(toSweep, ep)\\n70: \\t\\t}\\n71: \\t}\\n72: \\tmgr.mutex.RUnlock()\\n73: \\n74: \\tfor _, ep := range toSweep {\\n75: \\t\\tlog.WithFields(logrus.Fields{\\n76: \\t\\t\\tlogfields.EndpointID:  ep.StringID(),\\n77: \\t\\t\\tlogfields.ContainerID: ep.GetShortContainerID(),\\n78: \\t\\t\\tlogfields.K8sPodName:  ep.GetK8sNamespaceAndPodName(),\\n79: \\t\\t\\tlogfields.CEPName:     ep.GetK8sNamespaceAndCEPName(),\\n80: \\t\\t\\tlogfields.URL:         \\\"https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/86944\\\",\\n81: \\t\\t}).Warning(\\\"Stray endpoint found. You may be affected by upstream Kubernetes issue #86944.\\\")\\n82: \\t\\terrs := mgr.RemoveEndpoint(ep, endpoint.DeleteConfig{\\n83: \\t\\t\\tNoIPRelease: ep.DatapathConfiguration.ExternalIpam,\\n84: \\t\\t})\\n85: \\t\\tif len(errs) > 0 {\\n86: \\t\\t\\tscopedLog := log.WithField(logfields.EndpointID, ep.GetID())\\n87: \\t\\t\\tfor _, err := range errs {\\n88: \\t\\t\\t\\tscopedLog.WithError(err).Warn(\\\"Ignoring error while garbage collecting endpoint\\\")\\n89: \\t\\t\\t}\\n90: \\t\\t}\\n91: \\t}\\n92: }\\n93: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/cilium--v1.16.5@14bf8b70da6d30733726dd61ffc026d1ed2b187c/-/blob/pkg/endpointmanager/gc.go\"}","structuredContent":{"content":"1: // SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n2: // Copyright Authors of Cilium\n3: \n4: package endpointmanager\n5: \n6: import (\n7: \t\"context\"\n8: \n9: \t\"github.com/sirupsen/logrus\"\n10: \n11: \t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/endpoint\"\n12: \t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/logging/logfields\"\n13: )\n14: \n15: // EndpointCheckerFunc can verify whether an endpoint is currently healthy.\n16: type EndpointCheckerFunc func(*endpoint.Endpoint) error\n17: \n18: // markAndSweep performs a two-phase garbage collection of endpoints using the\n19: // configured EndpointChecker.\n20: //\n21: // 1) Mark all endpoints that require GC. Do not GC these endpoints this round.\n22: // 2) Sweep all endpoints marked as requiring GC during the previous iteration.\n23: //\n24: // This way, if there is a temporary condition that will be resolved by other\n25: // components in the system, then we will not flag warnings about the system\n26: // getting out-of-sync.\n27: func (mgr *endpointManager) markAndSweep(ctx context.Context) error {\n28: \tmarked := mgr.markEndpoints()\n29: \n30: \tmgr.mutex.Lock()\n31: \ttoSweep := mgr.markedEndpoints\n32: \tmgr.markedEndpoints = marked\n33: \tmgr.mutex.Unlock()\n34: \n35: \t// Avoid returning an error which would cause the calling controller to\n36: \t// re-run the garbage collection more frequently than the RunInterval.\n37: \tmgr.sweepEndpoints(toSweep)\n38: \treturn nil\n39: }\n40: \n41: // markEndpoints runs all endpoints in the manager against the configured\n42: // EndpointChecker and returns a slice of endpoint ids that require garbage\n43: // collection.\n44: func (mgr *endpointManager) markEndpoints() []uint16 {\n45: \tmgr.mutex.RLock()\n46: \tdefer mgr.mutex.RUnlock()\n47: \n48: \tneedsGC := make([]uint16, 0, len(mgr.endpoints))\n49: \tfor eid, ep := range mgr.endpoints {\n50: \t\tif err := mgr.checkHealth(ep); err != nil {\n51: \t\t\tneedsGC = append(needsGC, eid)\n52: \t\t}\n53: \t}\n54: \treturn needsGC\n55: }\n56: \n57: // sweepEndpoints iterates through the specified list of endpoints marked for\n58: // deletion and attempts to garbage-collect them if they still exist.\n59: func (mgr *endpointManager) sweepEndpoints(markedEndpoints []uint16) {\n60: \ttoSweep := make([]*endpoint.Endpoint, 0, len(markedEndpoints))\n61: \n62: \t// 'markedEndpoints' were marked during the previous mark round, so\n63: \t// they may no longer be valid endpoints. Narrow the list to only the\n64: \t// endpoints that remain. Then, release the lock so RemoveEndpoint()\n65: \t// below can independently grab it.\n66: \tmgr.mutex.RLock()\n67: \tfor _, id := range markedEndpoints {\n68: \t\tif ep, ok := mgr.endpoints[id]; ok {\n69: \t\t\ttoSweep = append(toSweep, ep)\n70: \t\t}\n71: \t}\n72: \tmgr.mutex.RUnlock()\n73: \n74: \tfor _, ep := range toSweep {\n75: \t\tlog.WithFields(logrus.Fields{\n76: \t\t\tlogfields.EndpointID:  ep.StringID(),\n77: \t\t\tlogfields.ContainerID: ep.GetShortContainerID(),\n78: \t\t\tlogfields.K8sPodName:  ep.GetK8sNamespaceAndPodName(),\n79: \t\t\tlogfields.CEPName:     ep.GetK8sNamespaceAndCEPName(),\n80: \t\t\tlogfields.URL:         \"https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/86944\",\n81: \t\t}).Warning(\"Stray endpoint found. You may be affected by upstream Kubernetes issue #86944.\")\n82: \t\terrs := mgr.RemoveEndpoint(ep, endpoint.DeleteConfig{\n83: \t\t\tNoIPRelease: ep.DatapathConfiguration.ExternalIpam,\n84: \t\t})\n85: \t\tif len(errs) > 0 {\n86: \t\t\tscopedLog := log.WithField(logfields.EndpointID, ep.GetID())\n87: \t\t\tfor _, err := range errs {\n88: \t\t\t\tscopedLog.WithError(err).Warn(\"Ignoring error while garbage collecting endpoint\")\n89: \t\t\t}\n90: \t\t}\n91: \t}\n92: }\n93: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/cilium--v1.16.5@14bf8b70da6d30733726dd61ffc026d1ed2b187c/-/blob/pkg/endpointmanager/gc.go"}}}

Keep it\n55: \t\t// below the default Prometheus scrape interval of 15s anyway.\n56: \t\tMinInterval: policyMapPressureMinInterval,\n57: \t\tTriggerFunc: func([]string) { p.update() },\n58: \t\tName:        \"endpointmanager-policymap-max-size-metrics\",\n59: \t})\n60: \tif err != nil {\n61: \t\tlog.WithError(err).Panic(\"Failed to initialize trigger for policymap pressure metric\")\n62: \t}\n63: \n64: \treturn p\n65: }\n66: \n67: func (mgr *policyMapPressure) update() {\n68: \tlog.Debug(\"EndpointManager policymap event metric update triggered\")\n69: \n70: \tif mgr.gauge == nil {\n71: \t\treturn\n72: \t}\n73: \n74: \tmgr.RLock()\n75: \tmax := float64(0)\n76: \tfor _, value := range maps.Values(mgr.current) {\n77: \t\tif value > max {\n78: \t\t\tmax = value\n79: \t\t}\n80: \t}\n81: \tmgr.RUnlock()\n82: \tmgr.gauge.Set(max)\n83: }\n84: \n85: type metricsGauge interface {\n86: \tSet(value float64)\n87: }\n88: \n89: // policyMapPressure implements policyMapPressure to provide the endpoint's\n90: // policymap pressure metric. It only exports the maximum policymap pressure\n91: // from all endpoints within the EndpointManager to reduce cardinality of the\n92: // metric.\n93: type policyMapPressure struct {\n94: \tlock.RWMutex\n95: \n96: \t// current holds the current maximum policymap pressure values by endpoint ID\n97: \t// that is pushed into gauge via trigger..\n98: \tcurrent map[uint16]float64\n99: \n100: \t// gauge is the gauge metric.\n101: \tgauge metricsGauge\n102: \n103: \t// trigger handles exporting / updating the gauge with the value in current\n104: \t// on an interval.\n105: \ttrigger *trigger.Trigger\n106: }\n107: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/cilium--v1.16.5@14bf8b70da6d30733726dd61ffc026d1ed2b187c/-/blob/pkg/endpointmanager/policymap_pressure.go"}}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01UQ2Mw5vs5xxGgPhcgzYbGK","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01FxCRSqFT5fFBGPsE2YZQ6r","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/workspace/pkg/endpointmanager/gc.go","content":"// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n// Copyright Authors of Cilium\n\npackage endpointmanager\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\n\t\"github.com/sirupsen/logrus\"\n\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/endpoint\"\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/logging/logfields\"\n)\n\n// EndpointCheckerFunc can verify whether an endpoint is currently healthy.\ntype EndpointCheckerFunc func(*endpoint.Endpoint) error\n\n// markAndSweep performs a two-phase garbage collection of endpoints using the\n// configured EndpointChecker.\n//\n// 1) Mark all endpoints that require GC. Do not GC these endpoints this round.\n// 2) Sweep all endpoints marked as requiring GC during the previous iteration.\n//\n// This way, if there is a temporary condition that will be resolved by other\n// components in the system, then we will not flag warnings about the system\n// getting out-of-sync.\nfunc (mgr *endpointManager) markAndSweep(ctx context.Context) error {\n\tmarked := mgr.markEndpoints()\n\n\tmgr.mutex.Lock()\n\ttoSweep := mgr.markedEndpoints\n\tmgr.markedEndpoints = marked\n\tmgr.mutex.Unlock()\n\n\t// Avoid returning an error which would cause the calling controller to\n\t// re-run the garbage collection more frequently than the RunInterval.\n\tmgr.sweepEndpoints(toSweep)\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// markEndpoints runs all endpoints in the manager against the configured\n// EndpointChecker and returns a slice of endpoint ids that require garbage\n// collection.\nfunc (mgr *endpointManager) markEndpoints() []uint16 {\n\tmgr.mutex.RLock()\n\tdefer mgr.mutex.RUnlock()\n\n\tneedsGC := make([]uint16, 0, len(mgr.endpoints))\n\tfor eid, ep := range mgr.endpoints {\n\t\tif err := mgr.checkHealth(ep); err != nil {\n\t\t\tneedsGC = append(needsGC, eid)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn needsGC\n}\n\n// sweepEndpoints iterates through the specified list of endpoints marked for\n// deletion and attempts to garbage-collect them if they still exist.\nfunc (mgr *endpointManager) sweepEndpoints(markedEndpoints []uint16) {\n\ttoSweep := make([]*endpoint.Endpoint, 0, len(markedEndpoints))\n\n\t// 'markedEndpoints' were marked during the previous mark round, so\n\t// they may no longer be valid endpoints. Narrow the list to only the\n\t// endpoints that remain. Then, release the lock so RemoveEndpoint()\n\t// below can independently grab it.\n\tmgr.mutex.RLock()\n\tfor _, id := range markedEndpoints {\n\t\tif ep, ok := mgr.endpoints[id]; ok {\n\t\t\ttoSweep = append(toSweep, ep)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tmgr.mutex.RUnlock()\n\n\tfor _, ep := range toSweep {\n\t\tlog.WithFields(logrus.Fields{\n\t\t\tlogfields.EndpointID:  ep.StringID(),\n\t\t\tlogfields.ContainerID: ep.GetShortContainerID(),\n\t\t\tlogfields.K8sPodName:  ep.GetK8sNamespaceAndPodName(),\n\t\t\tlogfields.CEPName:     ep.GetK8sNamespaceAndCEPName(),\n\t\t\tlogfields.URL:         \"https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/86944\",\n\t\t}).Warning(\"Stray endpoint found. Do not GC these endpoints this round.\n// 2) Sweep all endpoints marked as requiring GC during the previous iteration.\n//\n// This way, if there is a temporary condition that will be resolved by other\n// components in the system, then we will not flag warnings about the system\n// getting out-of-sync.\nfunc (mgr *endpointManager) markAndSweep(ctx context.Context) error {\n\tmarked := mgr.markEndpoints()\n\n\tmgr.mutex.Lock()\n\ttoSweep := mgr.markedEndpoints\n\tmgr.markedEndpoints = marked\n\tmgr.mutex.Unlock()\n\n\t// Avoid returning an error which would cause the calling controller to\n\t// re-run the garbage collection more frequently than the RunInterval.\n\tmgr.sweepEndpoints(toSweep)\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// markEndpoints runs all endpoints in the manager against the configured\n// EndpointChecker and returns a slice of endpoint ids that require garbage\n// collection.\nfunc (mgr *endpointManager) markEndpoints() []uint16 {\n\tmgr.mutex.RLock()\n\tdefer mgr.mutex.RUnlock()\n\n\tneedsGC := make([]uint16, 0, len(mgr.endpoints))\n\tfor eid, ep := range mgr.endpoints {\n\t\tif err := mgr.checkHealth(ep); err != nil {\n\t\t\tneedsGC = append(needsGC, eid)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn needsGC\n}\n\n// sweepEndpoints iterates through the specified list of endpoints marked for\n// deletion and attempts to garbage-collect them if they still exist.\nfunc (mgr *endpointManager) sweepEndpoints(markedEndpoints []uint16) {\n\ttoSweep := make([]*endpoint.Endpoint, 0, len(markedEndpoints))\n\n\t// 'markedEndpoints' were marked during the previous mark round, so\n\t// they may no longer be valid endpoints. Narrow the list to only the\n\t// endpoints that remain. Then, release the lock so RemoveEndpoint()\n\t// below can independently grab it.\n\tmgr.mutex.RLock()\n\tfor _, id := range markedEndpoints {\n\t\tif ep, ok := mgr.endpoints[id]; ok {\n\t\t\ttoSweep = append(toSweep, ep)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tmgr.mutex.RUnlock()\n\n\tfor _, ep := range toSweep {\n\t\tlog.WithFields(logrus.Fields{\n\t\t\tlogfields.EndpointID:  ep.StringID(),\n\t\t\tlogfields.ContainerID: ep.GetShortContainerID(),\n\t\t\tlogfields.K8sPodName:  ep.GetK8sNamespaceAndPodName(),\n\t\t\tlogfields.CEPName:     ep.GetK8sNamespaceAndCEPName(),\n\t\t\tlogfields.URL:         \"https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/86944\",\n\t\t}).Warning(\"Stray endpoint found. Keep it\n\t\t// below the default Prometheus scrape interval of 15s anyway.\n\t\tMinInterval: policyMapPressureMinInterval,\n\t\tTriggerFunc: func([]string) { p.update() },\n\t\tName:        \"endpointmanager-policymap-max-size-metrics\",\n\t})\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tlog.WithError(err).Panic(\"Failed to initialize trigger for policymap pressure metric\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn p\n}\n\nfunc (mgr *policyMapPressure) update() {\n\tlog.Debug(\"EndpointManager policymap event metric update triggered\")\n\n\tif mgr.gauge == nil {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tmgr.RLock()\n\tmax := float64(0)\n\tfor _, value := range maps.Values(mgr.current) {\n\t\tif value > max {\n\t\t\tmax = value\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tmgr.RUnlock()\n\tmgr.gauge.Set(max)\n}\n\ntype metricsGauge interface {\n\tSet(value float64)\n}\n\n// policyMapPressure implements policyMapPressure to provide the endpoint's\n// policymap pressure metric. It only exports the maximum policymap pressure\n// from all endpoints within the EndpointManager to reduce cardinality of the\n// metric.\ntype policyMapPressure struct {\n\tlock.RWMutex\n\n\t// current holds the current maximum policymap pressure values by endpoint ID\n\t// that is pushed into gauge via trigger..\n\tcurrent map[uint16]float64\n\n\t// gauge is the gauge metric.\n\tgauge metricsGauge\n\n\t// trigger handles exporting / updating the gauge with the value in current\n\t// on an interval.\n\ttrigger *trigger.Trigger\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":6,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2895,"cache_read_input_tokens":130898,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":2895},"output_tokens":3,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"fb31cf4e-f074-4a7f-a9b4-fdfbb0a837c8","uuid":"039527f2-a991-4a5f-9901-7a1e98ed20a7"}

Also exposed are EndpointsLookup and\n// EndpointsModify APIs that EndpointManager implements. If possible, choose\n// the minimal API as your dependency.\nvar Cell = cell.Module(\n\t\"endpoint-manager\",\n\t\"Manages the collection of local endpoints\",\n\n\tcell.Config(defaultEndpointManagerConfig),\n\tcell.Provide(newDefaultEndpointManager),\n\tcell.ProvidePrivate(newEndpointSynchronizer),\n)\n\ntype EndpointsLookup interface {\n\t// Lookup looks up endpoint by prefix ID\n\tLookup(id string) (*endpoint.Endpoint, error)\n\n\t// LookupCiliumID looks up endpoint by endpoint ID\n\tLookupCiliumID(id uint16) *endpoint.Endpoint\n\n\t// LookupCNIAttachmentID looks up endpoint by CNI attachment ID\n\tLookupCNIAttachmentID(id string) *endpoint.Endpoint\n\n\t// LookupIPv4 looks up endpoint by IPv4 address\n\tLookupIPv4(ipv4 string) *endpoint.Endpoint\n\n\t// LookupIPv6 looks up endpoint by IPv6 address\n\tLookupIPv6(ipv6 string) *endpoint.Endpoint\n\n\t// LookupIP looks up endpoint by IP address\n\tLookupIP(ip netip.Addr) (ep *endpoint.Endpoint)\n\n\t// LookupCEPName looks up endpoints by namespace + cep name, e.g. \"prod/cep-0\"\n\tLookupCEPName(name string) (ep *endpoint.Endpoint)\n\n\t// GetEndpointsByPodName looks up endpoints by namespace + pod name, e.g. \"prod/pod-0\"\n\tGetEndpointsByPodName(name string) []*endpoint.Endpoint\n\n\t// GetEndpointsByContainerID looks up endpoints by container ID\n\tGetEndpointsByContainerID(containerID string) []*endpoint.Endpoint\n\n\t// GetEndpoints returns a slice of all endpoints present in endpoint manager.\n\tGetEndpoints() []*endpoint.Endpoint\n\n\t// EndpointExists returns whether the endpoint with id exists.\n\tEndpointExists(id uint16) bool\n\n\t// GetHostEndpoint returns the host endpoint.\n\tGetHostEndpoint() *endpoint.Endpoint\n\n\t// HostEndpointExists returns true if the host endpoint exists.\n\tHostEndpointExists() bool\n\n\t// GetIngressEndpoint returns the ingress endpoint.\n\tGetIngressEndpoint() *endpoint.Endpoint\n\n\t// IngressEndpointExists returns true if the ingress endpoint exists.\n\tIngressEndpointExists() bool\n\n\t// GetEndpointNetnsCookieByIP returns the netns cookie for the passed endpoint with ip address if found.\n\tGetEndpointNetnsCookieByIP(ip netip.Addr) (uint64, error)\n}\n\ntype EndpointsModify interface {\n\t// AddEndpoint takes the prepared endpoint object and starts managing it.\n\tAddEndpoint(owner regeneration.Owner, ep *endpoint.Endpoint) (err error)\n\n\t// AddIngressEndpoint creates an Endpoint representing Cilium Ingress on this node without a\n\t// corresponding container necessarily existing. This is needed to be able to ingest and\n\t// sync network policies applicable to Cilium Ingress to Envoy.\n\tAddIngressEndpoint(\n\t\tctx context.Context,\n\t\towner regeneration.Owner,\n\t\tpolicyGetter policyRepoGetter,\n\t\tipcache *ipcache.IPCache,\n\t\tproxy endpoint.EndpointProxy,\n\t\tallocator cache.IdentityAllocator,\n\t) error\n\n\tAddHostEndpoint(\n\t\tctx context.Context,\n\t\towner regeneration.Owner,\n\t\tpolicyGetter policyRepoGetter,\n\t\tipcache *ipcache.IPCache,\n\t\tproxy endpoint.EndpointProxy,\n\t\tallocator cache.IdentityAllocator,\n\t) error\n\n\t// RestoreEndpoint exposes the specified endpoint to other subsystems via the\n\t// manager.\n\tRestoreEndpoint(ep *endpoint.Endpoint) error\n\n\t// UpdateReferences updates maps the contents of mappings to the specified endpoint.\n\tUpdateReferences(ep *endpoint.Endpoint) error\n\n\t// RemoveEndpoint stops the active handling of events by the specified endpoint,\n\t// and prevents the endpoint from being globally acccessible via other packages.\n\tRemoveEndpoint(ep *endpoint.Endpoint, conf endpoint.DeleteConfig) []error\n}\n\ntype EndpointManager interface {\n\tEndpointsLookup\n\tEndpointsModify\n\tEndpointResourceSynchronizer\n\n\t// Subscribe to endpoint events.\n\tSubscribe(s Subscriber)\n\n\t// Unsubscribe from endpoint events.\n\tUnsubscribe(s Subscriber)\n\n\t// UpdatePolicyMaps returns a WaitGroup which is signaled upon once all endpoints\n\t// have had their PolicyMaps updated against the Endpoint's desired policy state.\n\t//\n\t// Endpoints will wait on the 'notifyWg' parameter before updating policy maps.\n\tUpdatePolicyMaps(ctx context.Context, notifyWg *sync.WaitGroup) *sync.WaitGroup\n\n\t// RegenerateAllEndpoints calls a setState for each endpoint and\n\t// regenerates if state transaction is valid. During this process, the endpoint\n\t// list is locked and cannot be modified.\n\t// Returns a waiting group that can be used to know when all the endpoints are\n\t// regenerated.\n\tRegenerateAllEndpoints(regenMetadata *regeneration.ExternalRegenerationMetadata) *sync.WaitGroup\n\n\t// WaitForEndpointsAtPolicyRev waits for all endpoints which existed at the time\n\t// this function is called to be at a given policy revision.\n\t// New endpoints appearing while waiting are ignored.\n\tWaitForEndpointsAtPolicyRev(ctx context.Context, rev uint64) error\n\n\t// OverrideEndpointOpts applies the given options to all endpoints.\n\tOverrideEndpointOpts(om option.OptionMap)\n\n\t// InitHostEndpointLabels initializes the host endpoint's labels with the\n\t// node's known labels.\n\tInitHostEndpointLabels(ctx context.Context)\n\n\t// GetPolicyEndpoints returns a map of all endpoints present in endpoint\n\t// manager as policy.Endpoint interface set for the map key.\n\tGetPolicyEndpoints() map[policy.Endpoint]struct{}\n\n\t// HasGlobalCT returns true if the endpoints have a global CT, false otherwise.\n\tHasGlobalCT() bool\n\n\t// CallbackForEndpointsAtPolicyRev registers a callback on all endpoints that\n\t// exist when invoked. We want to retry without a\\n296: \\t\\t\\t\\t// backoff and the Update* calls returned the current localCEP\\n297: \\t\\t\\t\\tcase err != nil && k8serrors.IsConflict(err):\\n298: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tscopedLog.WithError(err).Warn(\\\"Cannot update CEP due to a revision conflict. The next controller execution will try again\\\")\\n299: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tneedInit = true\\n300: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil\\n301: \\n302: \\t\\t\\t\\t// Ensure we re-init when we see a generic error. This will recrate the\\n303: \\t\\t\\t\\t// CEP.\\n304: \\t\\t\\t\\tcase err != nil:\\n305: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// Suppress logging an error if ep backing the pod was terminated\\n306: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// before CEP could be updated and shut down the controller.\\n307: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif errors.Is(err, context.Canceled) {\\n308: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil\\n309: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n310: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tscopedLog.WithError(err).Error(\\\"Cannot update CEP\\\")\\n311: \\n312: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tneedInit = true\\n313: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn err\\n314: \\n315: \\t\\t\\t\\t// A successful update means no more updates unless the endpoint status, aka mdl, changes\\n316: \\t\\t\\t\\tdefault:\\n317: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tlastMdl = mdl\\n318: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil\\n319: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n320: \\t\\t\\t},\\n321: \\t\\t\\tStopFunc: func(ctx context.Context) error {\\n322: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn deleteCEP(ctx, scopedLog, ciliumClient, e)\\n323: \\t\\t\\t},\\n324: \\t\\t})\\n325: }\\n326: \\n327: // updateCEPUID attempts to update the endpoints UID to be that of localCEP.\\n328: // This in effect takes ownership of the referenced CEP, thus we can only\\n329: // do this if it is safe to do so. Otherwise an error is returned.\\n330: //\\n331: // One caveat is that, although endpoints are now restored to reference their\\n332: // previous CEP, this has to handle cases where agent was upgraded from a version\\n333: // that did not store CEP UIDs in the restore state header.\\n334: // It is only safe to do so if the CEP is local.\\n335: //\\n336: // In all cases where the endpoint cannot take ownership of a CEP, it is assumed\\n337: // that this is a temporary state where either the local/remote agent managing the CEP\\n338: // is shutting down and will delete the CEP, or the CEP is stale and needs to be cleaned\\n339: // up by the operator.\\n340: func updateCEPUID(scopedLog *logrus.Entry, e *endpoint.Endpoint, localCEP *cilium_v2.CiliumEndpoint) error {\\n341: \\t// It's possible we already own this CEP, as in the case of a restore after restart.\\n342: \\t// If the Endpoint already owns the CEP (by holding the matching CEP UID reference) then we don't have to\\n343: \\t// worry about other ownership checks.\\n344: \\t//\\n345: \\t// This will cover cases such as if the NodeIP changes (as with a reboot).\\n346: \\t// In which case we can safely take ownership and overwrite the CEPs\\n347: \\t// status. However if the cilium endpoints are lost on restart (eg the\\n348: \\t// state files were previously checkpointed into tmpfs) this check will\\n349: \\t// fail and we will rely on the next check to prevent us from hijacking\\n350: \\t// CEPs.\\n351: \\tcepUID := e.GetCiliumEndpointUID()\\n352: \\tif cepUID == localCEP.UID {\\n353: \\t\\treturn nil\\n354: \\t}\\n355: \\n356: \\t// We do not want to take ownership of CEPs created on other Nodes.\\n357: \\t// However we can't directly compare the CEP node ip with the node, because\\n358: \\t// the node ip can change, orphaning the CEP. So we retrieve the pod for\\n359: \\t// the CEP and compare its node IP with that of the node. The kubelet on\\n360: \\t// this node will update the pod object appropriately, allowing this check\\n361: \\t// to eventually go through.\\n362: \\t//\\n363: \\t// The intent here is to check if a given pod is running on the same node\\n364: \\t// this cilium is running on before taking over its CEP.\\n365: \\tcepOwner := e.GetCEPOwner()\\n366: \\tif cepOwner.IsNil() {\\n367: \\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"endpoint sync cannot take ownership of CEP: no pod\\\")\\n368: \\t}\\n369: \\tpodHostIP := cepOwner.GetHostIP()\\n370: \\tif podHostIP == \\\"\\\" {\\n371: \\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"endpoint sync cannot take ownership of CEP: no pod HostIP\\\")\\n372: \\t}\\n373: \\tif nodeIP := node.GetIPv4().String(); podHostIP != nodeIP {\\n374: \\t\\t// Also checking node ipv6 for k8s dual stack with ipv6 preference where\\n375: \\t\\t// podHostIP is gonna be node ipv6\\n376: \\t\\tif nodeIPV6 := node.GetIPv6().String(); podHostIP != nodeIPV6 {\\n377: \\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"endpoint sync cannot take ownership of CEP that is not local: CEP's pod %q, pod's hostIP %q, cilium nodeIP %q)\\\",\\n378: \\t\\t\\t\\te.GetK8sPodName(), podHostIP, nodeIP)\\n379: \\t\\t}\\n380: \\t}\\n381: \\n382: \\t// If the endpoint has a CEP UID, which does not match the current CEP, we cannot take\\n383: \\t// ownership.\\n384: \\tif cepUID != \\\"\\\" && cepUID != localCEP.GetUID() {\\n385: \\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"endpoint sync could not take ownership of CEP %q, endpoint UID (%q) did not match CEP UID: %q\\\",\\n386: \\t\\t\\tlocalCEP.GetNamespace()+\\\"/\\\"+localCEP.GetName(), cepUID, localCEP.GetUID())\\n387: \\t}\\n388: \\n389: \\tif cepUID := e.GetCiliumEndpointUID(); cepUID == \\\"\\\" {\\n390: \\t\\tscopedLog.WithFields(logrus.Fields{\\n391: \\t\\t\\tlogfields.Node:           types.GetName(),\\n392: \\t\\t\\t\\\"old\\\" + logfields.CEPUID: cepUID,\\n393: \\t\\t\\tlogfields.CEPUID:         localCEP.UID,\\n394: \\t\\t}).Debug(\\\"updating CEP UID and syncing endpoint header file\\\")\\n395: \\t\\te.SetCiliumEndpointUID(localCEP.UID)\\n396: \\t\\te.SyncEndpointHeaderFile()\\n397: \\t}\\n398: \\treturn nil\\n399: }\\n400: \\n401: // DeleteK8sCiliumEndpointSync replaces the endpoint controller to remove the\\n402: // CEP from Kubernetes once the endpoint is stopped / removed from the\\n403: // Cilium agent.\\n404: func (epSync *EndpointSynchronizer) DeleteK8sCiliumEndpointSync(e *endpoint.Endpoint) {\\n405: \\tcontrollerName := endpoint.EndpointSyncControllerName(e.ID)\\n406: \\n407: \\tscopedLog := e.Logger(subsysEndpointSync).WithField(\\\"controller\\\", controllerName)\\n408: \\n409: \\tif !epSync.Clientset.IsEnabled() {\\n410: \\t\\tscopedLog.Debug(\\\"Not starting controller because k8s is disabled\\\")\\n411: \\t\\treturn\\n412: \\t}\\n413: \\tciliumClient := epSync.Clientset.CiliumV2()\\n414: \\n415: \\t// The health endpoint doesn't really exist in k8s and updates to it caused\\n416: \\t// arbitrary errors. Disable the controller for these endpoints.\\n417: \\tif isHealthEP := e.HasLabels(pkgLabels.LabelHealth); isHealthEP {\\n418: \\t\\tscopedLog.Debug(\\\"Not starting unnecessary CEP controller for cilium-health endpoint\\\")\\n419: \\t\\treturn\\n420: \\t}\\n421: \\n422: \\t// NOTE: The controller functions do NOT hold the endpoint locks\\n423: \\te.UpdateController(controllerName,\\n424: \\t\\tcontroller.ControllerParams{\\n425: \\t\\t\\tGroup: ciliumEndpointToK8sSyncControllerGroup,\\n426: \\t\\t\\tStopFunc: func(ctx context.Context) error {\\n427: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn deleteCEP(ctx, scopedLog, ciliumClient, e)\\n428: \\t\\t\\t},\\n429: \\t\\t},\\n430: \\t)\\n431: }\\n432: \\n433: func deleteCEP(ctx context.Context, scopedLog *logrus.Entry, ciliumClient v2.CiliumV2Interface, e *endpoint.Endpoint) error {\\n434: \\tcepName := e.GetK8sCEPName()\\n435: \\tif cepName == \\\"\\\" {\\n436: \\t\\tscopedLog.Debug(\\\"Skipping CiliumEndpoint deletion because it has no k8s cep name\\\")\\n437: \\t\\treturn nil\\n438: \\t}\\n439: \\n440: \\tcepOwner := e.GetCEPOwner()\\n441: \\tif cepOwner.IsNil() {\\n442: \\t\\tscopedLog.Debug(\\\"Skipping CiliumEndpoint deletion because owner is nil\\\")\\n443: \\t\\treturn nil\\n444: \\t}\\n445: \\n446: \\t// A CEP should be only be deleted by the agent that manages the\\n447: \\t// corresponding pod. However, it is possible for a pod to restart and be\\n448: \\t// scheduled onto a different node while the agent on the original node was\\n449: \\t// down, which would cause the CEP to be deleted once the original agent came\\n450: \\t// back up. (This holds for StatefulSets in particular that come with stable\\n451: \\t// pod identifiers and thus do not guard against such accidental deletes\\n452: \\t// through unique pod names.) Storing the CEP UID at CEP create/fetch time\\n453: \\t// and using it as a precondition for deletion ensures that agents may only\\n454: \\t// delete CEPs they own.\\n455: \\t// It is possible for the CEP UID to not be populated when an agent tries to\\n456: \\t// clean up a CEP. EndpointSynchronizer struct {\\n44: \\tClientset client.Clientset\\n45: }\\n46: \\n47: // RunK8sCiliumEndpointSync starts a controller that synchronizes the endpoint\\n48: // to the corresponding k8s \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n224: \\t\\t\\t\\t// We have no localCEP copy. We need to fetch it for updates, below.\\n225: \\t\\t\\t\\t// This is unexpected as there should be only 1 writer per CEP, this\\n226: \\t\\t\\t\\t// controller, and the localCEP created on startup will be used.\\n227: \\t\\t\\t\\tif localCEP == nil {\\n228: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tlocalCEP, err = ciliumClient.CiliumEndpoints(cepOwner.GetNamespace()).Get(ctx, cepName, meta_v1.GetOptions{})\\n229: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tswitch {\\n230: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcase err == nil:\\n231: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// Backfill the CEP UID as we need to do if the CEP was\\n232: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// created on an agent version that did not yet store the\\n233: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// UID at CEP create time.\\n234: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif err := updateCEPUID(scopedLog, e, localCEP); err != nil {\\n235: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tscopedLog.WithError(err).Warn(\\\"could not take ownership of existing ciliumendpoint\\\")\\n236: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn err\\n237: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n238: \\n239: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// The CEP doesn't exist in k8s. This is unexpetected but may occur\\n240: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// if the endpoint was removed from k8s but not yet within the agent.\\n241: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// Mark the CEP for creation on the next controller iteration. This\\n242: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// may never occur if the controller is stopped on Endpoint delete.\\n243: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcase k8serrors.IsNotFound(err):\\n244: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tneedInit = true\\n245: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn err\\n246: \\n247: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// We cannot read the upstream CEP. needInit will cause the next\\n248: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// iteration to delete and create the CEP. This is an unexpected\\n249: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// situation.\\n250: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcase k8serrors.IsInvalid(err):\\n251: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tscopedLog.WithError(err).Warn(\\\"Invalid CEP during update\\\")\\n252: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tneedInit = true\\n253: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil\\n254: \\n255: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// A real error\\n256: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tdefault:\\n257: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tscopedLog.WithError(err).Error(\\\"Cannot get CEP during update\\\")\\n258: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn err\\n259: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n260: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n261: \\n262: \\t\\t\\t\\t// For json patch we don't need to perform a GET for endpoints\\n263: \\n264: \\t\\t\\t\\t// If it fails it means the test from the previous patch failed\\n265: \\t\\t\\t\\t// so we can safely replace this node in the CNP status.\\n266: \\t\\t\\t\\treplaceCEPStatus := []k8s.JSONPatch{\\n267: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// If the stored UID matches the one in the ciliumendpoint then\\n268: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// this first patch is a no-op, otherwise the entire patch will\\n269: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// not be applied as uid is immutable.\\n270: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t{\\n271: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tOP:    \\\"test\\\",\\n272: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tPath:  \\\"/metadata/uid\\\",\\n273: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tValue: e.GetCiliumEndpointUID(),\\n274: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t},\\n275: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t{\\n276: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tOP: []byte\\n282: \\t\\t\\t\\tcreateStatusPatch, err = json.Marshal(replaceCEPStatus)\\n283: \\t\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n284: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn err\\n285: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n286: \\n287: \\t\\t\\t\\tlocalCEP, err = ciliumClient.CiliumEndpoints(cepOwner.GetNamespace()).Patch(\\n288: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tctx, cepName,\\n289: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tk8stypes.JSONPatchType,\\n290: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcreateStatusPatch,\\n291: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tmeta_v1.PatchOptions{})\\n292: \\n293: \\t\\t\\t\\t// Handle Update errors or return successfully\\n294: \\t\\t\\t\\tswitch {\\n295: \\t\\t\\t\\t// Return no error when we see a conflict. We want to retry without a\\n296: \\t\\t\\t\\t// backoff and the Update* calls returned the current localCEP\\n297: \\t\\t\\t\\tcase err != nil && k8serrors.IsConflict(err):\\n298: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tscopedLog.WithError(err).Warn(\\\"Cannot update CEP due to a revision conflict. The next controller execution will try again\\\")\\n299: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tneedInit = true\\n300: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil\\n301: \\n302: \\t\\t\\t\\t// Ensure we re-init when we see a generic error. This will recrate the\\n303: \\t\\t\\t\\t// CEP.\\n304: \\t\\t\\t\\tcase err != nil:\\n305: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// Suppress logging an error if ep backing the pod was terminated\\n306: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// before CEP could be updated and shut down the controller.\\n307: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif errors.Is(err, context.Canceled) {\\n308: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil\\n309: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n310: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tscopedLog.WithError(err).Error(\\\"Cannot update CEP\\\")\\n311: \\n312: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tneedInit = true\\n313: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn err\\n314: \\n315: \\t\\t\\t\\t// A successful update means no more updates unless the endpoint status, aka mdl, changes\\n316: \\t\\t\\t\\tdefault:\\n317: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tlastMdl = mdl\\n318: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil\\n319: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n320: \\t\\t\\t},\\n321: \\t\\t\\tStopFunc: func(ctx context.Context) error {\\n322: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn deleteCEP(ctx, scopedLog, ciliumClient, e)\\n323: \\t\\t\\t},\\n324: \\t\\t})\\n325: }\\n326: \\n327: // updateCEPUID attempts to update the endpoints UID to be that of localCEP.\\n328: // This in effect takes ownership of the referenced CEP, thus we can only\\n329: // do this if it is safe to do so. Otherwise an error is returned.\\n330: //\\n331: // One caveat is that, although endpoints are now restored to reference their\\n332: // previous CEP, this has to handle cases where agent was upgraded from a version\\n333: // that did not store CEP UIDs in the restore state header.\\n334: // It is only safe to do so if the CEP is local.\\n335: //\\n336: // In all cases where the endpoint cannot take ownership of a CEP, it is assumed\\n337: // that this is a temporary state where either the local/remote agent managing the CEP\\n338: // is shutting down and will delete the CEP, or the CEP is stale and needs to be cleaned\\n339: // up by the operator.\\n340: func updateCEPUID(scopedLog *logrus.Entry, e *endpoint.Endpoint, localCEP *cilium_v2.CiliumEndpoint) error {\\n341: \\t// It's possible we already own this CEP, as in the case of a restore after restart.\\n342: \\t// If the Endpoint already owns the CEP (by holding the matching CEP UID reference) then we don't have to\\n343: \\t// worry about other ownership checks.\\n344: \\t//\\n345: \\t// This will cover cases such as if the NodeIP changes (as with a reboot).\\n346: \\t// In which case we can safely take ownership and overwrite the CEPs\\n347: \\t// status. However if the cilium endpoints are lost on restart (eg the\\n348: \\t// state files were previously checkpointed into tmpfs) this check will\\n349: \\t// fail and we will rely on the next check to prevent us from hijacking\\n350: \\t// CEPs.\\n351: \\tcepUID := e.GetCiliumEndpointUID()\\n352: \\tif cepUID == localCEP.UID {\\n353: \\t\\treturn nil\\n354: \\t}\\n355: \\n356: \\t// We do not want to take ownership of CEPs created on other Nodes.\\n357: \\t// However we can't directly compare the CEP node ip with the node, because\\n358: \\t// the node ip can change, orphaning the CEP. So we retrieve the pod for\\n359: \\t// the CEP and compare its node IP with that of the node. The kubelet on\\n360: \\t// this node will update the pod object appropriately, allowing this check\\n361: \\t// to eventually go through.\\n362: \\t//\\n363: \\t// The intent here is to check if a given pod is running on the same node\\n364: \\t// this cilium is running on before taking over its CEP.\\n365: \\tcepOwner := e.GetCEPOwner()\\n366: \\tif cepOwner.IsNil() {\\n367: \\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"endpoint sync cannot take ownership of CEP: no pod\\\")\\n368: \\t}\\n369: \\tpodHostIP := cepOwner.GetHostIP()\\n370: \\tif podHostIP == \\\"\\\" {\\n371: \\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"endpoint sync cannot take ownership of CEP: no pod HostIP\\\")\\n372: \\t}\\n373: \\tif nodeIP := node.GetIPv4().String(); podHostIP != nodeIP {\\n374: \\t\\t// Also checking node ipv6 for k8s dual stack with ipv6 preference where\\n375: \\t\\t// podHostIP is gonna be node ipv6\\n376: \\t\\tif nodeIPV6 := node.GetIPv6().String(); podHostIP != nodeIPV6 {\\n377: \\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"endpoint sync cannot take ownership of CEP that is not local: CEP's pod %q, pod's hostIP %q, cilium nodeIP %q)\\\",\\n378: \\t\\t\\t\\te.GetK8sPodName(), podHostIP, nodeIP)\\n379: \\t\\t}\\n380: \\t}\\n381: \\n382: \\t// If the endpoint has a CEP UID, which does not match the current CEP, we cannot take\\n383: \\t// ownership.\\n384: \\tif cepUID != \\\"\\\" && cepUID != localCEP.GetUID() {\\n385: \\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"endpoint sync could not take ownership of CEP %q, endpoint UID (%q) did not match CEP UID: %q\\\",\\n386: \\t\\t\\tlocalCEP.GetNamespace()+\\\"/\\\"+localCEP.GetName(), cepUID, localCEP.GetUID())\\n387: \\t}\\n388: \\n389: \\tif cepUID := e.GetCiliumEndpointUID(); cepUID == \\\"\\\" {\\n390: \\t\\tscopedLog.WithFields(logrus.Fields{\\n391: \\t\\t\\tlogfields.Node:           types.GetName(),\\n392: \\t\\t\\t\\\"old\\\" + logfields.CEPUID: cepUID,\\n393: \\t\\t\\tlogfields.CEPUID:         localCEP.UID,\\n394: \\t\\t}).Debug(\\\"updating CEP UID and syncing endpoint header file\\\")\\n395: \\t\\te.SetCiliumEndpointUID(localCEP.UID)\\n396: \\t\\te.SyncEndpointHeaderFile()\\n397: \\t}\\n398: \\treturn nil\\n399: }\\n400: \\n401: // DeleteK8sCiliumEndpointSync replaces the endpoint controller to remove the\\n402: // CEP from Kubernetes once the endpoint is stopped / removed from the\\n403: // Cilium agent.\\n404: func (epSync *EndpointSynchronizer) DeleteK8sCiliumEndpointSync(e *endpoint.Endpoint) {\\n405: \\tcontrollerName := endpoint.EndpointSyncControllerName(e.ID)\\n406: \\n407: \\tscopedLog := e.Logger(subsysEndpointSync).WithField(\\\"controller\\\", controllerName)\\n408: \\n409: \\tif !epSync.Clientset.IsEnabled() {\\n410: \\t\\tscopedLog.Debug(\\\"Not starting controller because k8s is disabled\\\")\\n411: \\t\\treturn\\n412: \\t}\\n413: \\tciliumClient := epSync.Clientset.CiliumV2()\\n414: \\n415: \\t// The health endpoint doesn't really exist in k8s and updates to it caused\\n416: \\t// arbitrary errors. Disable the controller for these endpoints.\\n417: \\tif isHealthEP := e.HasLabels(pkgLabels.LabelHealth); isHealthEP {\\n418: \\t\\tscopedLog.Debug(\\\"Not starting unnecessary CEP controller for cilium-health endpoint\\\")\\n419: \\t\\treturn\\n420: \\t}\\n421: \\n422: \\t// NOTE: The controller functions do NOT hold the endpoint locks\\n423: \\te.UpdateController(controllerName,\\n424: \\t\\tcontroller.ControllerParams{\\n425: \\t\\t\\tGroup: ciliumEndpointToK8sSyncControllerGroup,\\n426: \\t\\t\\tStopFunc: func(ctx context.Context) error {\\n427: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn deleteCEP(ctx, scopedLog, ciliumClient, e)\\n428: \\t\\t\\t},\\n429: \\t\\t},\\n430: \\t)\\n431: }\\n432: \\n433: func deleteCEP(ctx context.Context, scopedLog *logrus.Entry, ciliumClient v2.CiliumV2Interface, e *endpoint.Endpoint) error {\\n434: \\tcepName := e.GetK8sCEPName()\\n435: \\tif cepName == \\\"\\\" {\\n436: \\t\\tscopedLog.Debug(\\\"Skipping CiliumEndpoint deletion because it has no k8s cep name\\\")\\n437: \\t\\treturn nil\\n438: \\t}\\n439: \\n440: \\tcepOwner := e.GetCEPOwner()\\n441: \\tif cepOwner.IsNil() {\\n442: \\t\\tscopedLog.Debug(\\\"Skipping CiliumEndpoint deletion because owner is nil\\\")\\n443: \\t\\treturn nil\\n444: \\t}\\n445: \\n446: \\t// A CEP should be only be deleted by the agent that manages the\\n447: \\t// corresponding pod. However, it is possible for a pod to restart and be\\n448: \\t// scheduled onto a different node while the agent on the original node was\\n449: \\t// down, which would cause the CEP to be deleted once the original agent came\\n450: \\t// back up. (This holds for StatefulSets in particular that come with stable\\n451: \\t// pod identifiers and thus do not guard against such accidental deletes\\n452: \\t// through unique pod names.) Storing the CEP UID at CEP create/fetch time\\n453: \\t// and using it as a precondition for deletion ensures that agents may only\\n454: \\t// delete CEPs they own.\\n455: \\t// It is possible for the CEP UID to not be populated when an agent tries to\\n456: \\t// clean up a CEP. const (\n35: \t// subsysEndpointSync is the value for logfields.LogSubsys\n36: \tsubsysEndpointSync = \"endpointsynchronizer\"\n37: )\n38: \n39: var ciliumEndpointToK8sSyncControllerGroup = EndpointSynchronizer struct {\n44: \tClientset client.Clientset\n45: }\n46: \n47: // RunK8sCiliumEndpointSync starts a controller that synchronizes the endpoint\n48: // to the corresponding k8s CiliumEndpoint CRD. It is expected that each CEP\n49: // has 1 controller that updates it, and a local copy is retained and only\n50: // updates are pushed up.\n51: // CiliumEndpoint objects have the same name as the pod they represent.\n52: func (epSync *EndpointSynchronizer) RunK8sCiliumEndpointSync(e *endpoint.Endpoint, h cell.Health) {\n53: \tvar (\n54: \t\tendpointID     = e.ID\n55: \t\tcontrollerName = endpoint.EndpointSyncControllerName(endpointID)\n56: \t\tscopedLog      = e.Logger(subsysEndpointSync).WithFields(logrus.Fields{\n57: \t\t\t\"controller\": controllerName,\n58: \t\t\t\"endpointID\": e.ID,\n59: \t\t})\n60: \t)\n61: \n62: \tif option.Config.DisableCiliumEndpointCRD {\n63: \t\th.Stopped(\"ciliumendpoint CRD disabled\")\n64: \t\tscopedLog.Debug(\"Not running controller. CEP CRD synchronization is disabled\")\n65: \t\treturn\n66: \t}\n67: \n68: \tif !epSync.Clientset.IsEnabled() {\n69: \t\th.Stopped(\"k8s client-set disabled\")\n70: \t\tscopedLog.Debug(\"Not starting controller because k8s is disabled\")\n71: \t\treturn\n72: \t}\n73: \n74: \tciliumClient := epSync.Clientset.CiliumV2()\n75: \n76: \t// The health endpoint doesn't really exist in k8s and updates to it caused\n77: \t// arbitrary errors. It is compared with the one\n136: \t\t\t\t// from before, only updating on changes.\n137: \t\t\t\tmdl := e.GetCiliumEndpointStatus()\n138: \t\t\t\tif !needInit && mdl.DeepEqual(lastMdl) {\n139: Otherwise an error is returned.\n330: //\n331: // One caveat is that, although endpoints are now restored to reference their\n332: // previous CEP, this has to handle cases where agent was upgraded from a version\n333: // that did not store CEP UIDs in the restore state header.\n334: // It is only safe to do so if the CEP is local.\n335: //\n336: // In all cases where the endpoint cannot take ownership of a CEP, it is assumed\n337: // that this is a temporary state where either the local/remote agent managing the CEP\n338: // is shutting down and will delete the CEP, or the CEP is stale and needs to be cleaned\n339: // up by the operator.\n340: func updateCEPUID(scopedLog *logrus.Entry, e *endpoint.Endpoint, localCEP *cilium_v2.CiliumEndpoint) error {\n341: \t// It's possible we already own this CEP, as in the case of a restore after restart.\n342: \t// If the Endpoint already owns the CEP (by holding the matching CEP UID reference) then we don't have to\n343: \t// worry about other ownership checks.\n344: \t//\n345: \t// This will cover cases such as if the NodeIP changes (as with a reboot).\n346: \t// In which case we can safely take ownership and overwrite the CEPs\n347: \t// status. However if the cilium endpoints are lost on restart (eg the\n348: \t// state files were previously checkpointed into tmpfs) this check will\n349: \t// fail and we will rely on the next check to prevent us from hijacking\n350: \t// CEPs.\n351: \tcepUID := e.GetCiliumEndpointUID()\n352: \tif cepUID == localCEP.UID {\n353: \t\treturn nil\n354: \t}\n355: \n356: \t// We do not want to take ownership of CEPs created on other Nodes.\n357: \t// However we can't directly compare the CEP node ip with the node, because\n358: \t// the node ip can change, orphaning the CEP. So we retrieve the pod for\n359: \t// the CEP and compare its node IP with that of the node. The kubelet on\n360: \t// this node will update the pod object appropriately, allowing this check\n361: \t// to eventually go through.\n362: \t//\n363: \t// The intent here is to check if a given pod is running on the same node\n364: \t// this cilium is running on before taking over its CEP.\n365: \tcepOwner := e.GetCEPOwner()\n366: \tif cepOwner.IsNil() {\n367: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"endpoint sync cannot take ownership of CEP: no pod\")\n368: \t}\n369: \tpodHostIP := cepOwner.GetHostIP()\n370: \tif podHostIP == \"\" {\n371: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"endpoint sync cannot take ownership of CEP: no pod HostIP\")\n372: \t}\n373: \tif nodeIP := node.GetIPv4().String(); podHostIP != nodeIP {\n374: \t\t// Also checking node ipv6 for k8s dual stack with ipv6 preference where\n375: \t\t// podHostIP is gonna be node ipv6\n376: \t\tif nodeIPV6 := node.GetIPv6().String(); podHostIP != nodeIPV6 {\n377: \t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"endpoint sync cannot take ownership of CEP that is not local: CEP's pod %q, pod's hostIP %q, cilium nodeIP %q)\",\n378: \t\t\t\te.GetK8sPodName(), podHostIP, nodeIP)\n379: \t\t}\n380: \t}\n381: \n382: \t// If the endpoint has a CEP UID, which does not match the current CEP, we cannot take\n383: \t// ownership.\n384: \tif cepUID != \"\" && cepUID != localCEP.GetUID() {\n385: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"endpoint sync could not take ownership of CEP %q, endpoint UID (%q) did not match CEP UID: %q\",\n386: \t\t\tlocalCEP.GetNamespace()+\"/\"+localCEP.GetName(), cepUID, localCEP.GetUID())\n387: \t}\n388: \n389: \tif cepUID := e.GetCiliumEndpointUID(); cepUID == \"\" {\n390: \t\tscopedLog.WithFields(logrus.Fields{\n391: \t\t\tlogfields.Node:           types.GetName(),\n392: \t\t\t\"old\" + logfields.CEPUID: cepUID,\n393: \t\t\tlogfields.CEPUID:         localCEP.UID,\n394: \t\t}).Debug(\"updating CEP UID and syncing endpoint header file\")\n395: \t\te.SetCiliumEndpointUID(localCEP.UID)\n396: \t\te.SyncEndpointHeaderFile()\n397: \t}\n398: \treturn nil\n399: }\n400: \n401: // DeleteK8sCiliumEndpointSync replaces the endpoint controller to remove the\n402: // CEP from Kubernetes once the endpoint is stopped / removed from the\n403: // Cilium agent.\n404: func (epSync *EndpointSynchronizer) DeleteK8sCiliumEndpointSync(e *endpoint.Endpoint) {\n405: \tcontrollerName := endpoint.EndpointSyncControllerName(e.ID)\n406: \n407: \tscopedLog := e.Logger(subsysEndpointSync).WithField(\"controller\", controllerName)\n408: \n409: \tif !epSync.Clientset.IsEnabled() {\n410: \t\tscopedLog.Debug(\"Not starting controller because k8s is disabled\")\n411: \t\treturn\n412: \t}\n413: \tciliumClient := epSync.Clientset.CiliumV2()\n414: \n415: \t// The health endpoint doesn't really exist in k8s and updates to it caused\n416: \t// arbitrary errors. However, it is possible for a pod to restart and be\n448: \t// scheduled onto a different node while the agent on the original node was\n449: \t// down, which would cause the CEP to be deleted once the original agent came\n450: \t// back up. (This holds for StatefulSets in particular that come with stable\n451: \t// pod identifiers and thus do not guard against such accidental deletes\n452: \t// through unique pod names.)
